title: The traveler 2.0
body: Thanks to the Internet it is easy to get to know other places, we have information from anywhere in the world and we don't even have to leave home to get to know a multitude of cultures, landscapes and people. But there is nothing to go to the on-site site and yes, nowadays we have few vacations and to enjoy them we can prepare the trip beforehand. On the Internet we have a lot of information, sometimes too much, to start searching for flights and hotels it can be very tiresome, but thanks to websites like [VuelosBaratos](http://www.vuelosbaratos.es) or [Kayak](http://www.xarop.com/kayak/) we can select the best option among a multitude of providers. There are other more participatory networks such as TripAdvisor or WAYN, etc. But in my opinion, in addition to being sites basically in English, they are too complicated, I think we have to go one step further and get a really usable site. Many times these applications are very powerful, so much so that they are difficult to use, what must be achieved is that they are “intelligent” applications that, using various techniques, such as cookies or IPs, the website itself “suggests” our searches, as well as that these applications “learn” from users, that they are “living” programs that evolve with their use. In short, they are 2.0 websites for 2.0 travelers.
